title: Prepare access for pneumatic cylinder removal
summary: Safely prepare the tipper for pneumatic cylinder service by gathering the listed replacement part, PPE, and tools, applying lockout/tagout, removing the station side panel if needed, removing the gripper cover, and using Figure 7-10 to identify the pneumatic cylinder access area before continuing with further replacement work.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when preparing the OptiSweep tipper for pneumatic cylinder replacement or removal and access to the pneumatic cylinder area is required.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a complete pneumatic cylinder removal or installation procedure; the supplied source section is truncated and only supports preparation and access steps.
Do not use this runbook if lockout/tagout cannot be completed as referenced by the manual.
Do not use this runbook if the required replacement part, PPE, or listed tools are unavailable.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout and remove access covers and panels needed to reach the pneumatic cylinder area using the source-listed tools and PPE.

safety_notes: LOTO the tipper before starting work, as referenced by the manual.
Stop work if lockout/tagout cannot be completed.
This source-backed procedure only covers access preparation; do not proceed into unsupported removal or installation actions without additional source material.

system_preconditions: Tipper is available for maintenance access.
Required replacement part, PPE, and listed tools are available before starting.
external_references: Manual-referenced LOTO procedure for the tipper.

visual_references.description: Figure 7-10 maintenance diagram identifying the pneumatic cylinder and its access area for this procedure.

steps.title: Gather replacement part, PPE, and tools
steps.purpose: Ensure all required materials and tools are available before beginning access preparation work.
steps.instruction: Gather the replacement pneumatic cylinder, safety glasses, gloves, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, 24-mm open-ended wrench, 4-mm hex wrench, 5-mm hex wrench, and Loctite® before starting the procedure.
steps.expected_result: All listed parts, PPE, and tools are available at the work area.
steps.healthy_condition: The replacement pneumatic cylinder, PPE, and all listed tools are present and ready for use.
steps.failure_condition: Any required part, PPE item, or listed tool is missing or unavailable.

actions.label: Verify required materials and tools
actions.exact_action: Gather the listed replacement part, PPE, and tools: pneumatic cylinder, safety glasses, gloves, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, 24-mm open-ended wrench, 4-mm hex wrench, 5-mm hex wrench, and Loctite®.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the figure as the procedure visual reference associated with the pneumatic cylinder replacement task while preparing for access work.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the required PPE, replacement part, or listed tools are not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Nearby procedure lists required tools including a 3/8-in. wrench or socket, 24-mm open-ended wrench, 4-mm hex wrench, 5-mm hex wrench, and Loctite, along with PPE such as safety glasses and gloves.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe maintenance state before removing panels or covers.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper as referenced by the manual before starting work.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out and tagged out for maintenance.
steps.healthy_condition: LOTO has been completed in accordance with the referenced manual procedure before any access panels or covers are removed.
steps.failure_condition: LOTO cannot be completed or the tipper is not in a safe locked-out state.

actions.label: Apply LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper as referenced by the manual before starting work.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if lockout/tagout cannot be completed as referenced by the manual.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Nearby procedure steps instruct the technician to LOTO the tipper.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel if needed
steps.purpose: Open the side access area needed to reach internal components associated with the pneumatic cylinder service area.
steps.instruction: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel.
steps.expected_result: The station side panel is removed when needed for access.
steps.healthy_condition: Nine self-tapping fasteners are removed as needed and the station side panel is off, providing access to the internal area.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel cannot be removed, fasteners cannot be removed, or required access is still blocked.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine (9) self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel.

actions.label: Remove station side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the motor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use Figure 7-10 as the maintenance visual associated with the pneumatic cylinder area while opening access to the service location.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the side panel cannot be removed with the listed fasteners released.
Stop if access remains obstructed after the panel removal step.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Nearby procedure steps instruct the technician to remove nine self-tapping fasteners from the side panel if needed.
Figure 7-10 appears on page 148 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_10_1_pneumatic_cylinder_removal.

steps.title: Remove the gripper cover
steps.purpose: Open the gripper-side access needed to expose the pneumatic cylinder area.
steps.instruction: Remove the gripper cover by removing the eight (8) M8 button head screws.
steps.expected_result: The gripper cover is removed and the pneumatic cylinder access area is more exposed.
steps.healthy_condition: All eight M8 button head screws are removed and the gripper cover is off without blocking access.
steps.failure_condition: The gripper cover cannot be removed or the fasteners cannot be removed.

actions.label: Remove gripper cover screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the eight (8) M8 button head screws.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use Figure 7-10 to orient to the pneumatic cylinder mounting area and nearby access components after the gripper cover is removed.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the gripper cover cannot be removed after the eight M8 button head screws are removed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Nearby procedure steps instruct the technician to remove the gripper cover by taking out eight M8 button head screws.
Figure 7-10 appears on page 148 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_10_1_pneumatic_cylinder_removal.

steps.title: Identify the pneumatic cylinder access area using Figure 7-10
steps.purpose: Confirm the correct component and access area before continuing into unsupported removal or replacement work.
steps.instruction: Use Figure 7-10 to identify the pneumatic cylinder and its access area before continuing.
steps.expected_result: The pneumatic cylinder and its mounting/access area are visually identified.
steps.healthy_condition: The technician can clearly identify the pneumatic cylinder location and nearby access components using Figure 7-10.
steps.failure_condition: The pneumatic cylinder location cannot be confidently identified from the available source figure and opened access points.

actions.label: Identify pneumatic cylinder location
actions.exact_action: Use Figure 7-10 to identify the pneumatic cylinder and its access area before continuing.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Identify the pneumatic cylinder, its mounting area, and nearby access components behind the station side panel and gripper cover.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the pneumatic cylinder or its access area cannot be confidently identified.
Stop before proceeding into cylinder removal or installation because the provided source section does not include the full downstream procedure.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-10 appears on page 148 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_10_1_pneumatic_cylinder_removal.
Use this artifact when searching for the pneumatic cylinder location, identification, access points, and removal context during service.

success_criteria: The required replacement part, PPE, and listed tools have been gathered.
The tipper has been locked out and tagged out.
The station side panel has been removed if needed for access.
The gripper cover has been removed.
The pneumatic cylinder and its access area have been identified using Figure 7-10.
The pneumatic cylinder area is accessible for subsequent replacement work.
healthy_conditions: Required PPE, tools, and replacement pneumatic cylinder are available before work begins.
The tipper is in a locked-out state before panel or cover removal.
Access panels and covers are removed as needed without leaving the service area obstructed.
Figure 7-10 can be used to positively identify the pneumatic cylinder area.
failure_conditions: Lockout/tagout cannot be completed as referenced by the manual.
Required PPE, replacement part, or listed tools are not available.
The station side panel or gripper cover cannot be removed.
The pneumatic cylinder area cannot be confidently identified from the available figure and opened access points.
Further cylinder removal or installation details are needed but are not present in the supplied source packet.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if lockout/tagout cannot be completed as referenced by the manual.
Escalate if required PPE, replacement part, or listed tools are unavailable.
Escalate for additional source material or SME review before attempting any pneumatic cylinder removal or installation steps beyond access preparation.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference figure for pneumatic cylinder identification, mounting area, and nearby access components used throughout this access-preparation procedure.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-10 appears on page 148 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_10_1_pneumatic_cylinder_removal.

missing_details: The supplied source packet does not include the full pneumatic cylinder removal sequence.
The supplied source packet does not include the pneumatic cylinder installation sequence.
The source packet does not provide an estimated time for this procedure.
The source packet does not specify whether production stop is required beyond the LOTO requirement.
The source sections included in the packet contain no OCR text, so step wording is grounded primarily in the candidate and artifact retrieval text tied to page 148 and Figure 7-10.
